title: ctenopteris staheliana
intro: Ctenopteris staheliana, also known as the 'Australian Tree Fern', is a species of fern native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to have various health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Ctenopteris staheliana typically involve infusions or decoctions made from the plant's leaves or fronds, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
